old_claim.note: Herbal Medicine Preparation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No Abydos herbal-wine edge remains because the classical veterinary node is anchored to horse selection and care, not this Egyptian human-remedy residue.

source_shape.source_locator: The analyzed Abydos jar supports plant additives in grape wine but supplies no animal-treatment context or veterinary application.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The archaeochemical evidence establishes an Egyptian herbal-wine remedy without documenting care of working animals.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Possible use of plants in later animal care cannot substitute for a direct connection between the source-pinned recipe and Xenophon-era horsemanship.

ontology_before: A broad plant-remedy label was allowed to imply an unsupported veterinary treatment lineage.
ontology_after: The veterinary node follows its husbandry and cavalry-care evidence without inheriting the Abydos formulation.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: early_veterinary_medicine no longer directly depends on herbal_medicine_preparation.
would_reject_if: Direct evidence identified the Abydos preparation as an animal remedy or established its transmission into the scoped classical veterinary practice.
short_reason: The Abydos residue is not evidence for classical veterinary care.
